campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the city's business license tax rates for the upcoming fiscal year. Shannon Dragman from the Finance Department presented the proposed changes to the tax rates, and council members discussed and debated the new ordinance in public before voting on whether to approve it.
keyTopics: Business license tax rate increases
Fiscal year budget adjustments
Municipal code amendments
Public deliberation and council vote

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council voted to move forward with the proposal and scheduled it for final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked (first reading waived)
Ordinance scheduled for final vote on consent calendar at next meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, review the city's financial health for February, and authorize the town manager to sign several construction and service contracts. The council approved multiple agreements for building projects and professional services, though the full details of these contracts weren't fully specified in the agenda.
keyTopics: Approved February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with multiple companies
Approved professional services agreements (3-year term)
Approved previous meeting minutes from April 7, 2026

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 28, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several administrative items, including allowing free use of the Janet Gray Hayes Rotunda for a community welcoming luncheon event. The council also discussed pay and benefits agreements with park rangers and security initiatives for the Bay Area, along with reviewing policies about affordable housing restrictions.
keyTopics: Free use of City Hall Rotunda approved for 'Together San José' community luncheon
New pay and benefits agreement reached with city park rangers
Bay Area security initiative coordination with other agencies
Affordable housing protections and restrictions policies reviewed

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 28, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held a working meeting to review a draft plan for improving biodiversity and trees throughout Mountain View. They also recognized the Rengstorff Park Aquatics Center for winning a state award and declared April as Fair Housing Month in the city.
keyTopics: Draft Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan up for review
Rengstorff Park Aquatics Center receives California Parks and Recreation Society Award of Excellence
Fair Housing Month Proclamation
Plans to improve trees and wildlife habitat in the city

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents were able to participate both in person and online to watch the interviews and provide public comment.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
Public comment period for residents
In-person and online participation options

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 27, 2026 where residents were given time to speak about any city issues for up to 3 minutes each. The council also approved minutes from a January audit meeting and reviewed how well the city's retirement and health benefit investment accounts performed during the first quarter of 2026.
keyTopics: Public comment period for residents on any city matter
Approval of January 26 Audit Committee meeting minutes
Investment performance review for city employee pension and health benefit funds (Q1 2026)
State law limits council decisions on items not officially on the agenda

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The council also approved a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters covering 2026-2029.
keyTopics: Big advertising signs permitted near Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026
New contract agreement with Santa Clara firefighters (3-year deal)
One agenda item was withdrawn by staff before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held meetings to approve the city's next budget and various financial policies for 2026-27, and discussed plans to address homelessness in Santa Clara County. The council also reviewed and approved previous meeting minutes and scheduled upcoming meetings.
keyTopics: Approval of the 2026-27 city budget and development fees
Tax and fiscal responsibility policies (TEFRA reissuance)
Santa Clara County homelessness prevention and support plan
User fees and service charges updates

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed a request to reconsider a tree removal permit that was already approved for 751 Waverley Street. Someone asked the Planning Director to take another look at the decision to allow the removal of a protected tree on this property.
keyTopics: Protected tree removal permit at 751 Waverley Street
Request to reconsider an already-approved permit decision
Director's review of planning entitlement
